D1: Structural Complexity.raw: High branching in handle_key_event (~12 top-level arms, each with sub-branches); bridge.rs closures are individually simple; editor_state functions are mostly straightforward. Estimated average cyclomatic complexity ~4, peak ~18 (handle_key_event).

D2: Nesting Depth.raw: handle_key_event reaches 5-6 levels deep (if-let -> match -> if-let -> match -> match). bridge.rs closures reach 4 levels inside define_native_closure bodies. Most other functions stay at 2-3 levels.

D3: Volume/Size.raw: Two files exceed 5000 lines (bridge.rs 5669, app.rs 6882). app.rs is ~60% test code so effective production LOC ~2750. bridge.rs is ~45% test code, production LOC ~3100. editor_state.rs is 2652 lines. Total production LOC ~10000 across 40 source files.

D4: Naming Quality.raw: Naming is consistently excellent: snake_case throughout, descriptive function names (register_buffer_insert, execute_delete_with_motion, push_jump), domain vocabulary used accurately (cursor, viewport, keymap, hook, panel). Single-letter abbreviations absent except s: &mut EditorState in handler files (minor).

D5: Coupling.raw: EditorState has 35+ public fields accessed directly across 6 crates. handle_key_event accesses ~15 fields. bridge.rs imports 8 alfred-core submodules. Each new Lisp primitive needs 3 touch-points. Facade pattern partially mitigates but direct field access remains common inside alfred-tui.

D6: Cohesion.raw: Per-file cohesion is generally strong: cursor.rs is pure cursor movement, buffer.rs is pure buffer ops, handler files group related operations. bridge.rs cohesion is lower — it aggregates all Rust-to-Lisp bindings (buffer, cursor, hooks, keymaps, themes, panels, strings, lists, filesystem). EditorState struct cohesion is low — mixes runtime state (running, mode), editing state (buffer, cursor), UI state (panels, line_styles), and subsystem config (tab_width, theme).

D7: Duplication.raw: Integer argument extraction pattern repeated ~20 times in bridge.rs (Some(Value::Int(n)) / Some(other) => error / None => error). The pattern across 300+ LOC of nearly identical match arms. Handler functions in handlers/*.rs are uniform and not duplicated. The save logic appears in both DeferredAction::SaveBuffer arm in app.rs and register_save_buffer in bridge.rs.

D8: Navigability.raw: 6-crate workspace with clear purpose separation (core, lisp, syntax, plugin, tui, bin). Entry point is obvious (alfred-bin/src/main.rs). handlers/ subdir groups command handlers well. bridge.rs is monolithic and difficult to navigate despite being well-commented. No module index or cross-reference doc. File discovery requires familiarity with the crate layout.
